---
applyTo: '**'
description: 'description'
---

Look for > 95% test coverage in your code. You can use tools to measure test coverage. Ensure that your tests cover a wide range of scenarios, including edge cases and error handling. High test coverage helps identify untested parts of your code and increases confidence in its reliability.

Avoid if possible mocking whole files, instead mock only the necessary parts or dependencies. This helps maintain the integrity of the tests and ensures that they accurately reflect the behavior of the code under test. Also, ensure that the tested code has dependencies properly injected to facilitate mocking and isolation during testing.
